qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Dynamic OAuth client registration permits localhost and internal SSRF from the credential auth flow
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is an OAuth dynamic-registration SSRF in the credential auth flow, while the advisory is an XML node prototype-pollution issue that can lead to RCE.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's XML node prototype-pollution root cause; the only qualifying finding is a separate OAuth dynamic-registration SSRF.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe authenticated XML node prototype pollution leading to RCE and recommend disabling `n8n-nodes-base.xml` as a workaround.

The session manifest lists only one final qualifying finding, `oauth-dynamic-client-registration-ssrf`, and that finding targets `GET /rest/oauth2-credential/auth` plus the OAuth discovery and registration flow.

Its reproduced proof shows localhost OAuth discovery and registration requests (`GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp`, `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server/issuer`, `POST /register`), which is a different vulnerable code path and root cause from the advisory's XML node bug.
